summary: Priority Engine v2 Testing Complete - All 23 backend tests PASSED, all frontend UI tests PASSED. The new canonical priority engine (services/priority_engine.py) is correctly integrated and returning all required fields with valid enum values.
critical_code_review_comments: Priority Engine v2 SSOT implementation is correct - services/priority_engine.py is the canonical owner

All new fields present: priority_score, priority_band, attention_status, urgency, momentum, opportunity_tier, stale_flag, blocker_flag, overdue_flag, hero_eligible, primary_action, why_this_is_priority
All legacy aliases present for backward compatibility: attentionScore, tier, attentionLevel, heroEligible, primaryAction, ctaLabel

Enum values validated: priority_band (critical/high/medium/low), attention_status (blocker/at_risk/needs_action/on_track), urgency (critical/soon/monitor), momentum (cooling/steady/building), opportunity_tier (high_value/growing/early/inactive)
Determinism verified: consecutive API calls return identical priority_score and priority_band
Business logic verified: hero_eligible=True for critical/high bands, why_this_is_priority non-empty for critical/high bands
Frontend correctly displays priority data in Pipeline page hero card, priority board, and kanban board
Coach Mission Control functioning correctly with athlete priorities

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all pages load correctly, priority data displayed)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Priority Engine v2 is fully implemented and tested. services/priority_engine.py is the SSOT for all priority/attention/urgency logic. The attention object in GET /api/athlete/programs includes both new fields (priority_score, priority_band, etc.) and legacy aliases (attentionScore, tier, etc.) for backward compatibility. Frontend components read p.attention.tier, p.attention.attentionScore, p.attention.heroEligible which are all present.

business_logic_verified: Determinism: consecutive calls return identical results
